MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DECKER ELECTRIC CO., INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Seth Weisburst of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (415.986.5900, sweisburst@grsm.com) for defendant and moving party;
Daniel Feder of the Law Offices of Daniel Feder (415.391.9476, Daniel@dfederlaw.com) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court modifies its ruling as follows: the word fraud is stricken wherever it appears in the context of punitive damages. The tentative ruling is adopted in all other respects. The motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff J. Deryckx is denied. Deryckx has alleged sufficient ultimate facts to show both malice and oppression as those terms are defined in Civil Code 3294(c) (1) and (2). The allegations that Decker terminated Deryckx's employment to avoid the costs of Deryckx's required cancer treatment with the knowledge that depriving Deryckx of his health benefits would deprive Deryckx of the life-sustaining treatment is "despicable conduct": 1) "carried on ...with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of" Deryckx and 2) that subjects Deryckx "to cruel and unjust hardship in conscious disregard of" Deryckx's rights. The court did not address Decker's argument that Deryckx did not adequately allege managing agent liability since that argument was made for the first time in the reply memorandum. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
